gaps.title: Draft Composer Race Condition
gaps.breaks: Multiple simultaneous edits to the same draft cause data corruption
gaps.scenario: Agent A and B both edit draft #123 at the same time via Socket.io. Agent A saves, then Agent B saves over it, losing Agent A's changes permanently.
gaps.prompt: Write a test that connects two Socket.io clients to the same draft ID, have both clients send edit messages simultaneously with different content, verify only one edit is kept and the other is lost, then add retry logic to handle concurrent edits properly.

gaps.title: Training Data Cross-Contamination
gaps.breaks: Variant-specific data leaks between tenants in vector index
gaps.scenario: Tenant A's "support" variant gets trained on Tenant B's "sales" variant data due to incorrect tenant+variant metadata in the vector index, leading to privacy violations and incorrect responses.
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates two variants for different tenants with overlapping but different training data, query the vector index with tenant-specific queries, verify that results only contain data from the correct tenant-variant combination, and add isolation checks to the index query logic.

gaps.title: Edge Case Handler Ordering Vulnerability
gaps.breaks: Malicious input bypasses handlers by exploiting execution order
gaps.scenario: Attacker crafts a message with "malicious <script>alert(1)</script>" that passes the "HTML" handler before the "malicious HTML" handler due to incorrect ordering, executing XSS in the agent interface.
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates a message containing multiple edge case triggers in different orders, verify that all handlers execute regardless of order, and implement a priority-based execution system with proper input sanitization.

gaps.title: Socket.io Session Isolation Failure
gaps.breaks: Draft edits leak between concurrent agent sessions
gaps.scenario: Agent A starts editing draft #123, Agent B starts editing the same draft in a different session. Agent B sees Agent A's uncommitted changes in real-time before saving, violating workflow isolation.
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates two separate Socket.io sessions for the same draft ID with different agents, verify that edits in one session are not visible in the other until committed, and implement proper session isolation.

gaps.title: Cache Staleness Detection Bypass
gaps.breaks: Outdated drafts served despite freshness signals
gaps.scenario: Knowledge base is updated, freshness signal is sent but cache invalidation is delayed. Agent receives a draft generated with outdated KB data, leading to incorrect suggestions.
gaps.prompt: Write a test that updates the KB, sends the freshness signal, then immediately requests a draft, verify the draft uses updated data, and implement proper cache invalidation with timestamp checks.

gaps.title: Technique Tier Access Bypass
gaps.breaks: Higher-tier techniques accessible by lower-tier customers
gaps.scenario: A Starter tier customer crafts a request that triggers a High-tier technique by exploiting variant selection logic, accessing functionality they haven't paid for.
gaps.prompt: Write a test that attempts to access each technique tier with different customer tiers, verify that only appropriate techniques are accessible, and implement strict tier checks before technique selection.

gaps.title: Draft Regeneration State Loss
gaps.breaks: Failed regeneration leaves draft in inconsistent state
gaps.scenario: Agent clicks "regenerate" on draft #123, system fails midway through, draft remains partially updated with incomplete data. Agent can't recover original or new draft.
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a draft regeneration failure midway through the process, verify the system maintains the previous draft state or implements proper rollback, and adds error recovery mechanisms.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 7

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Draft Composer Race Condition
What breaks: Multiple simultaneous edits to the same draft cause data corruption
Real scenario: Agent A and B both edit draft #123 at the same time via Socket.io. Agent A saves, then Agent B saves over it, losing Agent A's changes permanently.
AI agent prompt: Write a test that connects two Socket.io clients to the same draft ID, have both clients send edit messages simultaneously with different content, verify only one edit is kept and the other is lost, then add retry logic to handle concurrent edits properly.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Training Data Cross-Contamination
What breaks: Variant-specific data leaks between tenants in vector index
Real scenario: Tenant A's "support" variant gets trained on Tenant B's "sales" variant data due to incorrect tenant+variant metadata in the vector index, leading to privacy violations and incorrect responses.
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two variants for different tenants with overlapping but different training data, query the vector index with tenant-specific queries, verify that results only contain data from the correct tenant-variant combination, and add isolation checks to the index query logic.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Edge Case Handler Ordering Vulnerability
What breaks: Malicious input bypasses handlers by exploiting execution order
Real scenario: Attacker crafts a message with "malicious <script>alert(1)</script>" that passes the "HTML" handler before the "malicious HTML" handler due to incorrect ordering, executing XSS in the agent interface.
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates a message containing multiple edge case triggers in different orders, verify that all handlers execute regardless of order, and implement a priority-based execution system with proper input sanitization.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Socket.io Session Isolation Failure
What breaks: Draft edits leak between concurrent agent sessions
Real scenario: Agent A starts editing draft #123, Agent B starts editing the same draft in a different session. Agent B sees Agent A's uncommitted changes in real-time before saving, violating workflow isolation.
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two separate Socket.io sessions for the same draft ID with different agents, verify that edits in one session are not visible in the other until committed, and implement proper session isolation.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: Cache Staleness Detection Bypass
What breaks: Outdated drafts served despite freshness signals
Real scenario: Knowledge base is updated, freshness signal is sent but cache invalidation is delayed. Agent receives a draft generated with outdated KB data, leading to incorrect suggestions.
AI agent prompt: Write a test that updates the KB, sends the freshness signal, then immediately requests a draft, verify the draft uses updated data, and implement proper cache invalidation with timestamp checks.

GAP 6
Severity: HIGH
Title: Technique Tier Access Bypass
What breaks: Higher-tier techniques accessible by lower-tier customers
Real scenario: A Starter tier customer crafts a request that triggers a High-tier technique by exploiting variant selection logic, accessing functionality they haven't paid for.
AI agent prompt: Write a test that attempts to access each technique tier with different customer tiers, verify that only appropriate techniques are accessible, and implement strict tier checks before technique selection.

GAP 7
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Draft Regeneration State Loss
What breaks: Failed regeneration leaves draft in inconsistent state
Real scenario: Agent clicks "regenerate" on draft #123, system fails midway through, draft remains partially updated with incomplete data. Agent can't recover original or new draft.
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a draft regeneration failure midway through the process, verify the system maintains the previous draft state or implements proper rollback, and adds error recovery mechanisms.
